speaker: SPEAKER_00
text: Welcome to the Rational Reminder Podcast. We're joined by Dr. Charles Chafin to discuss the psychology of financial planning and risk profiling.

speaker: SPEAKER_02
text: Psychology explains why and how clients behave with money, bridging the gap between technical advice and real-world behavior.

speaker: SPEAKER_02
text: Cognitive biases are shortcuts that create systematic errors like overconfidence or loss aversion, getting in the way of rational thinking.

speaker: SPEAKER_01
text: So how does our environment shape our financial psychology?

speaker: SPEAKER_02
text: Our environment shapes our financial psychology. We can create friction for unwanted behaviors and eliminate friction for goals.

speaker: SPEAKER_02
text: Money scripts are unconscious beliefs about money, like being money avoidant or money status, that impact our decisions.

speaker: SPEAKER_02
text: Setting goals aligns our behavior and motivates us. We're neurologically wired for progress, which gives us dopamine.

speaker: SPEAKER_02
text: Risk tolerance is our emotional response to uncertainty: willingness to endure short-term loss for potential long-term gain.

speaker: SPEAKER_02
text: Common risk assessments fall short by ignoring emotions and beliefs. MRI provides a holistic view of comfort with risk.

speaker: SPEAKER_00
text: Thanks Charles, this has been a great conversation.
